Professional Development

Courses, finance resources, and technical learning tied to my career direction.

I use online learning to build practical skills around construction finance, project controls, accounting fundamentals, analytics, and AI-enabled workflow improvement. These are displayed as professional development resources unless separately marked as completed credentials.

AI & Automation Learning resource

NVIDIA AI Courses

A technical AI learning track focused on understanding how modern AI tools can support productivity, automation, and applied problem-solving.

Why it matters for me: I want to use AI practically inside project accounting and operations workflows — summarizing documents, organizing invoice detail, creating reporting templates, and reducing repetitive manual work.
  • AI fundamentals
  • Workflow automation
  • Productivity systems
View source
Finance Fundamentals Reference guide

Cash Flow Guide

A finance resource centered on cash flow, timing of money, business liquidity, and how operating activity connects to financial health.

Why it matters for me: Cash flow is directly relevant to construction projects, requisitions, pay applications, vendor timing, and understanding how project costs move through a budget.
  • Cash flow
  • Project economics
  • Budget awareness
View source
AI Foundations Open course

Stanford AI Open Courses

A structured AI education resource that builds a stronger foundation in machine learning concepts, technical vocabulary, and responsible use of AI systems.

Why it matters for me: This gives me the language and baseline knowledge to understand where AI can fit into construction finance, reporting, and internal process improvement.
  • Machine learning basics
  • AI literacy
  • Technical fluency
View source
Analytics & Reporting Video learning

Top Data Analytics Videos

A practical analytics learning set focused on understanding data workflows, reporting logic, dashboards, and how to communicate insights clearly.

Why it matters for me: My background already includes live sports data and operations reporting. This helps me translate that into stronger finance reporting, Excel/Power BI work, and project controls analysis.
  • Data analysis

Accounting Fundamentals Study reference

Accounting Formulas

A fundamentals resource covering core accounting formulas and financial relationships used to understand how transactions affect business records.

Why it matters for me: This supports the accounting side of my current role — requisitions, invoice review, budget tracking, cost coding, and understanding how project financials tie together.
  • Accounting logic
  • Financial statements
  • Cost tracking
View source
Accounting Fundamentals Study resource

Basics of Accounting

A beginner-to-intermediate accounting resource that reinforces debits, credits, account classifications, financial statements, and transaction flow.

Why it matters for me: I’m sharpening the accounting foundation behind my project accounting work so I can speak more confidently with finance, construction, and project controls teams.
  • Debits and credits
  • Statements
  • Project accounting

Reading & Film

How I think outside the resume.

Books and film have helped shape how I think about markets, communication, discipline, risk, storytelling, and long-term career growth. This section gives recruiters a more complete view of my interests without taking the site out of a professional lane.

Film & Storytelling

Letterboxd watchlist and film interests

I use film as a way to study character, pressure, ambition, loyalty, risk, and decision-making. My taste leans toward crime dramas, historical epics, sports stories, financial ambition, and character-driven films.

  • The Godfather
  • Good Will Hunting
  • Interstellar
  • Slumdog Millionaire
  • The Departed
  • Goodfellas
  • Notting Hill
  • Whiplash
  • La Haine
View Letterboxd Lists

Burton Malkiel

My takeaway: Malkiel made markets feel less like gambling and more like discipline. The lesson I take from him is that long-term thinking, diversification, and humility matter more than pretending you can outsmart every cycle.

Dale Carnegie

My takeaway: Carnegie’s work is a reminder that communication is not just polish — it is a professional skill. Listening well, making people feel respected, and staying composed are advantages in client work, construction teams, and office politics.

Phil Knight

My takeaway: Knight’s story connects with me because it shows how messy real ambition is. Building something worthwhile usually starts with uncertainty, rejection, persistence, and a willingness to keep moving before everything looks perfect.

Bookshelf

Goodreads reading list

My reading interests include markets, investing, communication, leadership, history, sports, film, discipline, and personal development.
